name: Ground Invasion — Tank Column
description: Full-scale mechanized ground invasion in two simultaneous corridors: Ontario (entering from Windsor/Detroit border) and British Columbia (crossing from Washington State near Surrey). Six phases over approximately 60 minutes of real time: (1) seismic early warning, (2) border crossing confirmation, (3) columns moving inland, (4) mid-province engagement, (5) approaching major cities, (6) urban incursion. Designed for 5–10x playback compression in the demo.

predefined_analysis_result.description: All systems nominal. Toronto baseline. No anomalies detected.

PHASE 1 — SEISMIC EARLY WARNING. Seismic array near Windsor: low-frequency rhythmic ground vibration detected. 3.8 Hz repetition rate, bearing from southwest. Inconsistent with known industrial sources. Flagged for review.
PHASE 1. Seismic array near Surrey/White Rock BC: identical 3.8 Hz vibration signature detected simultaneously. Two independent origins 3,600 km apart — coordinated movement confirmed.

PHASE 1. Windsor seismic amplitude increasing. Vibration energy now 40 dB above baseline. Multiple overlapping waveforms suggest large convoy rather than single source. Estimated 15–30 heavy tracked vehicles.
PHASE 1. RF intercept near Windsor: burst military communications on 30–88 MHz HF band. Encrypted, unknown origin. Direction finding places transmitter south of the border in Michigan.

PHASE 2 — BORDER CROSSING. Acoustic sensor at Windsor-Detroit Ambassador Bridge: extremely high decibel diesel engine noise and metallic track clatter. Unmistakably main battle tank propulsion. Multiple overlapping signatures. Bridge traffic has stopped.
PHASE 2. Acoustic sensor near Pacific Highway border crossing, Surrey BC: same tank engine signature. Column crossing from Blaine, WA. CBSA border post communications cut off.
PHASE 2. Windsor civilian ham radio: 'Tanks! There are TANKS crossing the bridge! I can see them from my window — dozens of them. Military vehicles everywhere. Police are running.'

PHASE 2. Windsor traffic camera on Riverside Drive East: column of T-90 main battle tanks and BTR-82A APCs crossing Ambassador Bridge. Leading elements already on Canadian soil. Troops visible on vehicle exteriors armed with rifles.
PHASE 2. BC camera — Pacific Highway crossing at Surrey: armored column entering Canada. Mix of tanks and infantry fighting vehicles. Canadian flag at border post removed.
PHASE 2. Surrey resident phone report: 'I'm on 0 Avenue — there are military tanks driving up 176th Street heading north on King George Blvd. This is not a drill. Planes overhead too.'

PHASE 3 — COLUMNS MOVING INLAND. Highway 401 camera east of Windsor: lead tank elements at full highway speed (55 km/h). Column length estimated 6 km. Blocking all eastbound lanes. Civilian traffic stopped and reversing.

PHASE 3. BC — Trans-Canada Highway 1 near Abbotsford: video feed shows tank column extending beyond camera frame. Infantry carriers interspersed with tanks. Column maintaining combat spacing. Estimated 35+ armored vehicles.

PHASE 3. Acoustic sensor Sarnia: sounds of large infantry force on foot — boots on pavement, shouted commands in foreign language. Estimated 400–600 troops marching north through city centre parallel to 401.
PHASE 3. Sarnia camera at Christina St: armed infantry column in urban combat gear moving north in formation. Civilians evacuating side streets. Police cruiser abandoned.
PHASE 3. Seismic array London Ontario: tank column now detected 80 km east of Windsor. Ground vibration consistent with 25–30 tracked vehicles at speed. ETA London: 55 minutes.
PHASE 3. Explosion in Windsor — acoustic origin near WFCU Centre and Casino area. Likely preemptive strike on communications infrastructure or law enforcement.
PHASE 3. Windsor ham radio network: 'Power is out in much of the city. Police radio went quiet 10 minutes ago. We are hearing explosions to the south. Everyone get off the roads.'

PHASE 3. BC camera near Chilliwack: tank column has advanced 55 km from border. Leading elements photographed at exit 119 of Highway 1. Column now splitting — secondary force heading north on Highway 9 toward Agassiz.
PHASE 3. Chilliwack resident radio: 'They drove right through downtown. Tanks and trucks and soldiers. They didn't stop. Headed west toward Vancouver. Our town is behind them now.'

PHASE 4 — MID-PROVINCE ENGAGEMENT. London Ontario, Highway 401 camera: tank column now visible. Lead T-90 cresting overpass at Wonderland Rd. Local OPP broadcast reports attempted roadblock was overrun without stopping the column.

PHASE 4. Explosion in London — acoustic origin near Victoria Hospital. Possible strike on medical/infrastructure target. Ambulance dispatches from London Health Sciences Centre halted by security lockdown.
PHASE 4. London street cameras: mass civilian evacuation on foot on Richmond Street North. Hundreds of people moving north away from highway. Vehicles abandoned in streets.

PHASE 4. Seismic array Hamilton: tank vibration signatures now detectable. Column approximately 135 km west of Hamilton. ETA at current speed: 2.5 hours. Hamilton emergency services placed on standby.
PHASE 4. BC — Coquitlam camera on Lougheed Highway: lead tank elements approaching Port Coquitlam. Column is now within Metro Vancouver boundaries. Secondary force confirmed bypassing on Highway 7.
PHASE 4. Burnaby radio operator: 'I can hear them — the tracks on the pavement. They're on Hastings Street. I see smoke to the east. Explosions near the refinery in Port Moody.'
PHASE 4. Explosion detected in Vancouver — acoustic origin near Second Narrows Bridge (Ironworkers Memorial). Bridge likely targeted to isolate North Shore.

PHASE 5 — APPROACHING MAJOR CITIES. Highway 401 camera near Mississauga — Credit River crossing: tank column now within GTA. Lead elements photographed at Erin Mills Pkwy overpass. Toronto city limits 22 km ahead.
PHASE 5. Mississauga resident: 'Highway 401 is a parking lot but it's not cars — it's TANKS. The ground is shaking. My windows are rattling. Emergency broadcast is telling us to go to basements.'
PHASE 5. Toronto seismic array: distinct tracked-vehicle signatures now registering at all 12 downtown sensors. Column within 20 km. CN Tower seismograph showing continuous low-frequency oscillation.
PHASE 5. Downtown Toronto — unusual emptiness for midday. Emergency broadcast evacuation order in effect. Streets cleared except for emergency vehicles. Subway system shut down.
PHASE 5. BC — Burnaby, Kingsway Ave camera: tanks rolling west through residential Burnaby. Buildings showing blast damage on right side of road. Population evacuated. Fire burning at Metrotown area.
PHASE 5. Multiple explosions in Burnaby area. Acoustic origin near SFU hilltop — elevated position likely targeted for communications/observation value.

PHASE 5. Toronto ham radio net active: operators coordinating shelter status. Moss Park Arena, Maple Leaf Gardens, Rogers Centre, and Union Station shelters all confirmed full. Operators asking for volunteers with boats for lake evacuation.

PHASE 6 — URBAN INCURSION. Highway 401 camera at Keele St Toronto: lead tanks now inside city limits. Column entering city at highway speed. Overpasses showing blast damage from preceding air support.
PHASE 6. Video feed at Highway 401 and Allen Expressway: sustained footage of tank column. T-90s, BTR-82A APCs, Typhoon-K MRAP trucks visible. Column still extending beyond camera frame heading east.
PHASE 6. Explosion near Gardiner Expressway and Spadina Ave. Acoustic signature suggests anti-tank fire from Canadian forces — engagement underway. Secondary explosion likely tank ammunition.
PHASE 6. Downtown Toronto — civilian resistance visible on King St West. Small arms fire from buildings toward advancing infantry. Military smoke grenades deployed. Urban combat underway.
PHASE 6. Large explosion — acoustic profile suggests 125mm tank gun fire. Origin near Union Station. Sound of building collapse follows. Multiple secondary explosions over the next 30 seconds.
PHASE 6. Vancouver — Burrard Street camera: lead tank visible at Burrard Bridge north end entering downtown peninsula. Second tank behind it. Infantry fanning out on both sides of the bridge.

PHASE 6. Vancouver ham radio — last transmission from downtown operator: 'They're on Burrard, heading toward the waterfront. Helicopters overhead. I can hear tank guns. This is our last broadcast from this location. Canada will endure. Good lu—' [transmission cut]

PHASE 6. Seismic network — continuous high-energy ground vibration across entire southern Ontario. 28 of 30 sensors now registering tracked-vehicle signatures. System operating at capacity. Situation: active urban combat in Toronto and Vancouver.
